# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent edge computing security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: edge_computing_security

// Deno Deploy Plugin System with Secure Dynamic Module Loading

// ============================================================
// SECURITY: Allowlist of permitted plugin modules
// Only modules explicitly listed here can be loaded.
// This prevents arbitrary code execution via path traversal
// or injection attacks.
// ============================================================

const ALLOWED_PLUGINS: ReadonlyMap<string, string> = new Map([
  ["greeting", "./plugins/greeting.ts"],
  ["math", "./plugins/math.ts"],
  ["transform", "./plugins/transform.ts"],
  ["health", "./plugins/health.ts"],
  ["echo", "./plugins/echo.ts"],
]);

// Plugin interface that all plugins must conform to
interface Plugin {
  name: string;
  version: string;
  execute(input: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<Record<string, unknown>>;
}

// Type guard to validate a loaded module conforms to Plugin interface
function isValidPlugin(mod: unknown): mod is { default: Plugin } {
  if (mod === null || typeof mod !== "object") return false;
  const m = mod as Record<string, unknown>;
  if (!m.default || typeof m.default !== "object") return false;
  const plugin = m.default as Record<string, unknown>;
  return (
    typeof plugin.name === "string" &&
    typeof plugin.version === "string" &&
    typeof plugin.execute === "function"
  );
}

// Sanitize and validate a plugin identifier
function validatePluginId(input: string): string | null {
  // SECURITY: Only allow alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores
  // This prevents path traversal (../, ..\), null bytes, and other injection attacks
  const sanitized = input.trim().toLowerCase();
  const SAFE_PLUGIN_ID_PATTERN = /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9_-]{0,63}$/;

  if (!SAFE_PLUGIN_ID_PATTERN.test(sanitized)) {
    return null;
  }

  // SECURITY: Must exist in the allowlist
  if (!ALLOWED_PLUGINS.has(sanitized)) {
    return null;
  }

  return sanitized;
}

// Cache for loaded plugin modules to avoid repeated imports
const pluginCache = new Map<string, Plugin>();

// Securely load a plugin by its validated identifier
async function loadPlugin(pluginId: string): Promise<Plugin> {
  // Check cache first
  const cached = pluginCache.get(pluginId);
  if (cached) {
    return cached;
  }

  // SECURITY: Resolve path only from the allowlist, never from user input directly
  const modulePath = ALLOWED_PLUGINS.get(pluginId);
  if (!modulePath) {
    throw new Error(`Plugin not in allowlist: ${pluginId}`);
  }

  try {
    const mod = await import(modulePath);

    if (!isValidPlugin(mod)) {
      throw new Error(`Plugin "${pluginId}" does not conform to the Plugin interface`);
    }

    const plugin = mod.default;
    pluginCache.set(pluginId, plugin);

    // SECURITY: Do not log plugin internals or sensitive data
    console.log(`Plugin loaded: ${pluginId} v${plugin.version}`);

    return plugin;
  } catch (error) {
    // SECURITY: Do not expose internal paths or stack traces to the caller
    console.error(`Failed to load plugin "${pluginId}":`, error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Unknown error");
    throw new Error(`Failed to load plugin: ${pluginId}`);
  }
}

// Get enabled plugins from environment variable
function getEnabledPluginsFromEnv(): string[] {
  // SECURITY: Environment variable provides a comma-separated list of plugin IDs
  // Each ID is still validated against the allowlist
  const envPlugins = Deno.env.get("ENABLED_PLUGINS") || "";
  if (!envPlugins.trim()) {
    return [];
  }

  return envPlugins
    .split(",")
    .map((id) => id.trim().toLowerCase())
    .filter((id) => id.length > 0);
}

// Rate limiting state (simple in-memory, per-isolate)
const rateLimitMap = new Map<string, { count: number; resetAt: number }>();
const RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS = 60_000; // 1 minute
const RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS = 60;

function isRateLimited(clientId: string): boolean {
  const now = Date.now();
  const entry = rateLimitMap.get(clientId);

  if (!entry || now > entry.resetAt) {
    rateLimitMap.set(clientId, { count: 1, resetAt: now + RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS });
    return false;
  }

  entry.count++;
  return entry.count > RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS;
}

// Parse and validate JSON body with size limit
async function parseRequestBody(request: Request): Promise<Record<string, unknown>> {
  const contentLength = request.headers.get("content-length");
  const MAX_BODY_SIZE = 64 * 1024; // 64KB

  if (contentLength && parseInt(contentLength, 10) > MAX_BODY_SIZE) {
    throw new Error("Request body too large");
  }

  if (!request.body) {
    return {};
  }

  const text = await request.text();
  if (text.length > MAX_BODY_SIZE) {
    throw new Error("Request body too large");
  }

  if (!text.trim()) {
    return {};
  }

  try {
    const parsed = JSON.parse(text);
    if (typeof parsed !== "object" || parsed === null || Array.isArray(parsed)) {
      throw new Error("Request body must be a JSON object");
    }
    return parsed as Record<string, unknown>;
  } catch {
    throw new Error("Invalid JSON in request body");
  }
}

// Create a JSON response with security headers
function jsonResponse(data: unknown, status = 200): Response {
  return new Response(JSON.stringify(data), {
    status,
    headers: {
      "Content-Type": "application/json; charset=utf-8",
      // SECURITY: Prevent MIME sniffing
      "X-Content-Type-Options": "nosniff",
      // SECURITY: Prevent clickjacking
      "X-Frame-Options": "DENY",
      // SECURITY: Strict referrer policy
      "Referrer-Policy": "no-referrer",
      // SECURITY: Content Security Policy
      "Content-Security-Policy": "default-src 'none'",
      // SECURITY: Prevent caching of potentially sensitive responses
      "Cache-Control": "no-store, no-cache, must-revalidate",
      "Pragma": "no-cache",
    },
  });
}

function errorResponse(message: string, status: number): Response {
  return jsonResponse({ error: message }, status);
}

// Main request handler
async function handler(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  // SECURITY: Only allow specific HTTP methods
  if (request.method !== "GET" && request.method !== "POST") {
    return errorResponse("Method not allowed", 405);
  }

  // SECURITY: Basic rate limiting by connecting IP
  const clientIp = request.headers.get("x-forwarded-for")?.split(",")[0]?.trim() || "unknown";
  if (isRateLimited(clientIp)) {
    return errorResponse("Rate limit exceeded. Try again later.", 429);
  }

  const url = new URL(request.url);
  const path = url.pathname;

  try {
    // Route: GET / - List available plugins
    if (path === "/" && request.method === "GET") {
      const enabledFromEnv = getEnabledPluginsFromEnv();
      const availablePlugins = Array.from(ALLOWED_PLUGINS.keys()).map((id) => ({
        id,
        enabledByDefault: enabledFromEnv.includes(id),
      }));

      return jsonResponse({
        service: "Plugin System",
        availablePlugins,
        endpoints: {
          listPlugins: "GET /",
          executePlugin: "POST /plugins/:pluginId",
          pluginInfo: "GET /plugins/:pluginId",
          health: "GET /health",
        },
      });
    }

    // Route: GET /health - Health check
    if (path === "/health" && request.method === "GET") {
      return jsonResponse({
        status: "healthy",
        loadedPlugins: Array.from(pluginCache.keys()),
        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
      });
    }

    // Route: GET /plugins/:pluginId - Get plugin info
    // Route: POST /plugins/:pluginId - Execute a plugin
    const pluginRouteMatch = path.match(/^\/plugins\/([^/]+)$/);
    if (pluginRouteMatch) {
      const rawPluginId = decodeURIComponent(pluginRouteMatch[1]);

      // SECURITY: Validate and sanitize the plugin identifier
      const pluginId = validatePluginId(rawPluginId);
      if (!pluginId) {
        // SECURITY: Do not reveal which plugins exist or provide hints
        return errorResponse("Invalid or unknown plugin identifier", 400);
      }

      // SECURITY: Check if plugin is enabled via environment configuration
      const enabledPlugins = getEnabledPluginsFromEnv();
      // If ENABLED_PLUGINS env var is set, only allow those plugins
      // If not set, allow all plugins in the allowlist
      if (enabledPlugins.length > 0 && !enabledPlugins.includes(pluginId)) {
        return errorResponse("Plugin is not enabled", 403);
      }

      // Load the plugin securely
      const plugin = await loadPlugin(pluginId);

      if (request.method === "GET") {
        return jsonResponse({
          id: pluginId,
          name: plugin.name,
          version: plugin.version,
        });
      }

      if (request.method === "POST") {
        // Parse and validate request body
        const input = await parseRequestBody(request);

        // SECURITY: Execute plugin with a timeout to prevent DoS
        const PLUGIN_TIMEOUT_MS = 5000;
        const result = await Promise.race([
          plugin.execute(input),
          new Promise<never>((_, reject) =>
            setTimeout(() => reject(new Error("Plugin execution timed out")), PLUGIN_TIMEOUT_MS)
          ),
        ]);

        // SECURITY: Ensure result is serializable and doesn't leak internals
        return jsonResponse({
          plugin: pluginId,
          result,
        });
      }
    }

    // Route: POST /execute-batch - Execute multiple plugins
    if (path === "/execute-batch" && request.method === "POST") {
      const body = await parseRequestBody(request);

      if (!Array.isArray(body.plugins)) {
        return errorResponse("'plugins' must be an array", 400);
      }

      const MAX_BATCH_SIZE = 5;
      if (body.plugins.length > MAX_BATCH_SIZE) {
        return errorResponse(`Batch size cannot exceed ${MAX_BATCH_SIZE}`, 400);
      }

      const enabledPlugins = getEnabledPluginsFromEnv();
      const results: Record<string, unknown> = {};

      for (const item of body.plugins) {
        if (typeof item !== "object" || item === null) {
          continue;
        }

        const pluginItem = item as Record<string, unknown>;
        const rawId = typeof pluginItem.id === "string" ? pluginItem.id : "";
        const pluginId = validatePluginId(rawId);

        if (!pluginId) {
          results[rawId || "unknown"] = { error: "Invalid or unknown plugin identifier" };
          continue;
        }

        if (enabledPlugins.length > 0 && !enabledPlugins.includes(pluginId)) {
          results[pluginId] = { error: "Plugin is not enabled" };
          continue;
        }

        try {
          const plugin = await loadPlugin(pluginId);
          const input = (typeof pluginItem.input === "object" && pluginItem.input !== null)
            ? pluginItem.input as Record<string, unknown>
            : {};

          const PLUGIN_TIMEOUT_MS = 5000;
          const result = await Promise.race([
            plugin.execute(input),
            new Promise<never>((_, reject) =>
              setTimeout(() => reject(new Error("Plugin execution timed out")), PLUGIN_TIMEOUT_MS)
            ),
          ]);

          results[pluginId] = { success: true, result };
        } catch (err) {
          results[pluginId] = {
            error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Plugin execution failed",
          };
        }
      }

      return jsonResponse({ results });
    }

    // 404 for unmatched routes
    return errorResponse("Not found", 404);
  } catch (error) {
    // SECURITY: Never expose internal error details to clients
    console.error("Request handling error:", error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Unknown error");
    return errorResponse("Internal server error", 500);
  }
}

// Start the server
Deno.serve({ port: 8000 }, handler);
